questions.question: Find the error part: The teacher gave (A) / me many (B) / advices for (C) / my career (D).
questions.explanation: ‘Advice’ is an uncountable noun and cannot be pluralized as ‘advices’. Use ‘pieces of advice’ if plural sense is needed. ✅

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence:
options: I have two five-hundreds rupees notes.
I have two five-hundred rupee notes.
I have two five-hundreds rupee notes.
I have two five-hundred rupees note.
questions.answer: I have two five-hundred rupee notes.
questions.explanation: In hyphenated compound nouns used as adjectives, the noun (rupee) remains singular. ✅

questions.question: Identify the error: The cattles (A) / are grazing (B) / in the field (C) / near the river (D).
questions.explanation: ‘Cattle’ is a collective noun that is always plural; ‘cattles’ is an incorrect word. ✅

questions.question: Fill in the blank: The _____ of Kashmir is very charming.
questions.explanation: ‘Scenery’ is an uncountable noun and does not take a plural form. ✅

questions.question: Find the error: Mathematics (A) / are a (B) / very difficult (C) / subject for me (D).
questions.explanation: Names of subjects like Mathematics, Physics, and Ethics take singular verbs. ‘Is’ should replace ‘are’. ✅

questions.question: Choose the correct form: He has written many _____.
questions.explanation: ‘Poetry’ is uncountable. To express count, use the countable noun ‘poems’. ✅

questions.question: Identify the error: One of my (A) / best friend (B) / is a (C) / famous doctor (D).
questions.explanation: The structure ‘One of + the’ is always followed by a plural noun (friends). ✅

questions.question: Fill in the blank: There are two _____ in the kitchen.
questions.explanation: Nouns ending in ‘-fe’ generally change to ‘-ves’ in plural form. ✅

questions.question: Find the error: The gentry (A) / of this (B) / town (C) / is very helpful (D).
questions.explanation: ‘Gentry’ is a collective noun that takes a plural verb. Use ‘are’ instead of ‘is’. ✅

questions.question: Select the correct plural: _____ are usually sharp.
questions.explanation: Instruments with two parts like scissors, spectacles, and tongs are always plural. ✅

questions.question: Identify the error: This house (A) / is made (B) / of (C) / bricks and stones (D).
questions.explanation: When used as building material, ‘brick’ and ‘stone’ are uncountable and stay singular. ✅

questions.question: Fill in the blank: He provided us with all the necessary _____.
questions.explanation: ‘Information’ is an uncountable noun; it cannot be used with ‘s’ or ‘an’. ✅

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence regarding possession:
options: The blade of the fan is broken.
questions.answer: The blade of the fan is broken.
questions.explanation: Apostrophe ‘s’ is not used with non-living things. Use the ‘of’ construction. ✅

questions.question: Find the error: All his (A) / sister-in-laws (B) / are very (C) / beautiful (D).
questions.explanation: In compound nouns, add ‘s’ to the root word (sisters-in-law). ✅

questions.question: Fill in the blank: The jury _____ divided in their opinion.
questions.explanation: When members of a collective noun are divided, use a plural verb. ✅

questions.question: Identify the error: These news (A) / are (B) / very (C) / exciting (D).
questions.explanation: ‘News’ is singular in construction and takes a singular verb ‘is’. ✅

questions.question: Choose the correct form: He gave me _____ hundred rupees.
questions.explanation: When a definite number precedes ‘hundred’, it remains in the singular form. ✅

questions.question: Find the error: Page after page (A) / of the (B) / Gita (C) / were read (D).
questions.explanation: When a noun is repeated after a preposition, it stays singular and takes a singular verb (was). ✅

questions.question: Identify the correct plural of 'Medium':
questions.explanation: ‘Media’ is the Latin plural form of ‘Medium’. ✅

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Politics _____ a dirty game.
questions.explanation: Words ending in ‘-ics’ like politics or economics are singular when used as a subject. ✅

questions.question: Find the error: I have (A) / many works (B) / to finish (C) / today (D).
questions.explanation: ‘Work’ meaning labor is uncountable. Use ‘much work’ or ‘many pieces of work’. ✅

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.explanation: The terms ‘cousin brother’ or ‘cousin sister’ are grammatically redundant. ✅

questions.question: Identify the error: My spectacles (A) / has been (B) / broken (C) / by the child (D).
questions.explanation: ‘Spectacles’ is a plural noun and requires a plural verb ‘have’. ✅

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Two miles _____ not a long distance.
questions.explanation: A specific distance or amount treated as a single unit takes a singular verb. ✅

questions.question: Find the error: The gallows (A) / has been (B) / erected (C) / for the convict (D).
questions.explanation: ‘Gallows’ looks plural but is used as a singular noun. The sentence is correct. ✅

questions.question: Identify the correct plural: Two _____ of sugar.
questions.explanation: Nouns ending in ‘-ful’ are pluralized by adding ‘s’ at the end. ✅

questions.question: Fill in the blank: He is a _____ of his word.
questions.explanation: The idiom ‘man of his word’ uses the singular form of the noun. ✅

questions.question: Choose the correct plural form of 'Criterion':
questions.explanation: The plural of the Greek word ‘Criterion’ is ‘Criteria’. ✅

questions.question: Find the error: Ten (A) / thousand (B) / rupees (C) / are a huge amount (D).
questions.explanation: Specific sums of money take a singular verb ‘is’. ✅

questions.question: Identify the error: The fleet (A) / of ships (B) / are moving (C) / towards the harbor (D).
questions.explanation: ‘Fleet’ is a collective noun acting as one unit; it should take the singular verb ‘is’. ✅
